questions.question: All of the following are markers in case of liver cell injury, except:
Which is an indicator for liver synthetic function?
A patient with a gallstone that obstruct the common bile duct will present with which of the following?
questions.correct_answer: Positive Iodine test in urine and Clay coloured stool.
wrong_answers: Negative Iodine test in urine and Clay coloured stool.
Positive Iodine test in urine and Normal coloured stool.
Positive Erlich test in urine and Normal coloured stool

questions.question: Elevated levels of which enzyme are most indicative of liver injury?
Which of the following is the most common cause hyperbilirubinemia in newborns?
What is the primary cause of elevated alkaline phosphatase levels?
Elevated levels of which are typically associated with bile duct obstruction?
Which condition is indicated by the presence of "Jaundice"?
Elevated levels of "lactate dehydrogenase" (LDH) are associated with:
In the setting of severe liver disease, which test is most useful in assessing the degree of hepatocellular damage?
In unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia the followings are present:
questions.correct_answer: ↑ Serum unconjugated bilirubin, deep yellow urine with positive Ehrlich teste, normal colored stools.
wrong_answers: ↑ serum unconjugated bilirubin, deep yellow urine with negative Ehrlich teste, normal colored stools
↑ serum unconjugated bilirubin, deep yellow urine with Positive Ehrlich teste, clay colored stools
↑ serum unconjugated bilirubin, deep yellow urine with negative Ehrlich test, clay colored stools

questions.question: The best single test to assess hepatic synthetic function in acute conditions is measurement of:
What is the main difference between Alanine aminotransferase (ALT) and Gamma glutamyl transferase (GGT):
questions.correct_answer: It is more liver specific for detecting hepatocellular damage
wrong_answers: It increases significantly up to levels 10 to 12-fold in hepatobiliary obstruction
Elevated in hepatobiliary obstruction but usually normal in hepatocellular affection
Is elevated in cases of intra more than extra hepatic biliary obstruction

questions.question: Which of the following tests is positive in a patient with chronic active hepatitis B?
Which of the following enzymes is usually normal in hepatocellular damage?
Which enzyme is more specific for hepatocellular injury?
What is the indication of: total +ve Anti Hbc and +ve Hbs Antigen
What is the indication of: +ve Anti Hbc and +ve Anti Hbs
What is the indication of: Hbe Antigen
What is the indication of: Anti Hbs level < 10 mlU/mL
wrong_answers: Receive one additional dose of vaccine
